title: Isoloma tydaeum
taxonomy: Isoloma tydaeum is classified within the family Hymenophyllaceae. This family comprises of ferns belonging to the division Pteridophyta. Isoloma tydaeum is further grouped under the genus Isoloma, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. The genus Isoloma is native to Asia, with species found in tropical regions. Isoloma tydaeum's classification is further detailed within the subclass Polypodiidae, under the class Polypodiopsida.

intro:
Unfortunately, I was unable to verify any information about the plant Isoloma tydaeum.

However, I can provide information on a plant that is often confused with Isoloma, Isodon, or Isodon tyloeum which is also sometimes referred to as Isodon tyloeum or sometimes spelled Isoloma tyloeum, or sometimes Isodon tyloeum, but it's actually Isodon is a genus of flowering plants in the family Lamiaceae. 

Isodon (Isodon tyloeum) is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The health benefits of Isodon (Isodon tyloeum) include its use as an anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic agent, as well as its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Isodon (Isodon tyloeum) include diterpenoid quinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Isodon (Isodon tyloeum) include teas, extracts, and infusions that are used to treat a range of ailments.
